title: ulmus minor var. cornubiensis
intro: Ulmus minor var. cornubiensis, also known as the Cornish elm or dwarf elm, is a variety of the common elm tree (Ulmus minor) native to the southwestern region of England, specifically in Cornwall. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Ulmus minor var. cornubiensis include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
